project_name: DH2 Dunkirk — Green Hydrogen for Steel (France)
status_detail: 400 MW to 1 GW green hydrogen project in Dunkirk, France, by DH2 Energy and partners. Targeting steel decarbonization supply to ArcelorMittal Dunkirk. Pre-FEED stage. Part of the Dunkirk ZIBAC low-carbon industrial zone.

data_management.narrative_summary: DH2 Dunkirk is a large-scale green hydrogen project in the Dunkirk industrial zone, France, developed by DH2 Energy (the same company behind HyDeal Espana). The project targets up to 1 GW of electrolysis capacity to supply green hydrogen primarily for steel decarbonization (ArcelorMittal Dunkirk) and other industrial users in the ZIBAC low-carbon industrial zone. The project benefits from Dunkirk's existing industrial hydrogen demand (the zone consumes approximately 185,000 tonnes of hydrogen per year, mostly fossil-derived) and its deep-water port infrastructure for potential hydrogen and ammonia export. Pre-FEED studies are underway with a target of supplying competitively priced green hydrogen to industrial users.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected at Dunkirk port industrial zone
power.ppa_structure: Dedicated renewable PPAs to be secured

offtake.offtaker_name: ArcelorMittal Dunkirk, other ZIBAC industrial cluster members

sources.title: DH2 Energy plans multi-GW green hydrogen project in Dunkirk industrial zone
sources.author: Hydrogen Insight / FuelCellChina
